lessons.title: What money actually is
lessons.summary: Money isn't what most people think it is — and understanding the truth changes how you use it.

lessons.title: How banks create money
lessons.summary: Banks don't just store your money — they multiply it. Here's the mechanism that runs the entire financial world.

lessons.title: The 50/30/20 rule
lessons.summary: The simplest budgeting framework in existence — and how to adapt it to a Kenyan salary.

lessons.title: Compound interest — the eighth wonder
lessons.summary: The one concept that makes the difference between being comfortable at 50 and struggling — and why starting today matters more than starting with more.

lessons.title: Good debt vs bad debt
lessons.summary: Not all debt is the enemy. The difference between debt that builds wealth and debt that destroys it.

lessons.title: Inflation — why your KES 1,000 shrinks
lessons.summary: Inflation is the invisible tax on your savings. Understanding it changes how you think about holding cash forever.

lessons.title: The emergency fund — the boring secret
lessons.summary: The most unsexy financial move that protects every other financial decision you'll ever make.

lessons.title: Net worth — how to measure yourself
lessons.summary: Your salary tells you what you earn. Your net worth tells you where you actually stand.

series.name: Adult life money
series.tagline: Everything you'll pay for that nobody told you about.

series.name: Smart money
series.tagline: How the people who move markets actually think.

series.name: Kenya money
series.tagline: The local financial landscape nobody maps for you.

series.name: Teenager to adult
series.tagline: The guide you should have been given at 18.

series.name: Psychology of money
series.tagline: Why smart people make terrible money decisions.

series.name: Home & household money
series.tagline: Renting, owning, and running a home without going broke.

series.name: Investing & saving
series.tagline: Making your money work while you sleep.

series.name: Family & parenting money
series.tagline: Raising financially smart children — and a stable home.

download_system.description: Each lesson is a standalone markdown file. Users download individual lessons on demand. Downloaded lessons are stored locally and indexed for RAG retrieval. Users can delete completed lessons to free space.
